english: 3. W. wife, partner, spouse, *'á-ne kúr-če* ('a ne kur ce) to take a wife, to marry, *(s)kyá-wo 'á-ne kúr čog* ((s)kya wo 'a ne kur cog) a layman is at liberty to marry; *'á-ne-la čó-če* ('a ne la co ce) to treat, to use as a wife, sensu obsc. = to sleep with. — 4. a woman, a female. — 5. Sch. an old woman (?).
= 'á-pa ('a pa).
Ü: building (= *kar-lén* (kar len) W.), *'á-po gyáb-pa* ('a po gyab pa) to construct a house, to build.
Sch. zizel, earless marmot, souslik (Spermophilus citillus).

col. for p'a (pha), in B. of rare occurrence; *'á-pa čén-po* ('a pa cen po) the elder, *čúṅ-ṅu* (cung ngu) the younger, of the husbands of a person's mother, hence occasionally = uncle; *'á-pe śa* ('a pe sha) W. a vulgar oath; also (like p'a (pha)) uncastrated male animal, cf. p'a (pha).
C. col.: I, cf. k'o-bo-ṅó (kho bo ngo).
Mil, *'a-p'í* ('a phyi), *'a-pi* ('a pi), for p'yí-mo (phyi mo) grandmother.
old woman, goody, dame Sch.
Sch.: the bosom of a garment, 'a-p'rág-tu sdú-ba ('a phrag tu sdu ba) to put into the bosom, = 'am-bág ('am bag).
v. dhu-ti (dhu ti).
for baṅ-po (bang po), the husband of the father's or the mother's sister Cs.
Ssk.: 'antivenomous', n. of a medicinal herb Wdn.
1. Sch. = 'a-jo ('a jo). — 2. a medicine S.g.
Sch.: 'good, tolerable, middling', cf. Bun.: eb-bo (eb bo) good.
and 'a-bras ('a bras) names of medicines Med.
col. and sometimes in B. = ma (ma) mother; *'á-mẹ śa* ('a me sha) a vulgar oath; 'á-ma drín-čén ('a ma drin cen) so a king addresses a wonder-working nun Pth.
Cs. interj., prob. similar to a-gyis (a gyis).
the usual Tibetan corruption of अमिताभ, v. 'od-dpag-mẹd ('od dpag med).
Ssk. the mango tree and -fruit Dzl.
(Ssk. अमृत ambrosia; also various fruits etc.), in the Lt. perh. the guava fruit, which in Hindoostani is now called amrút.

interj. expressive of pain by touching hot objects Sch.; also used in various other instances, when disagreeably surprised, startled etc. bdag ma gról-la 'dí 'á-tsa-ma (bdag ma grol la 'di 'a tsa ma) alas! I am not yet released! Thgr.; 'á-tsa-ma yi-dwags snyíṅ re-rjé ('a tsa ma yi dwags snying re rje) alas! ye poor Yidags folk! Mil.
Mil. a species of hobgoblins, or spectres; in C. a Bengalee, acc. to Lew. The observations of Huc (II, 271) concerning this word seem to be mixed with some errors.
Ssk. आचार्य, spiritual guide or father, instructor, professor, doctor.
an interj. expressing discomfort occasioned by heat.
Sch.: oh dear, what a wonder!

Wdn., 'á-dzi-na ('a dzi na) Stg., अजिन Will.: the hairy skin of a black antelope, which serves the religious student for a couch, seat, covering etc.; Tibetan writers use it for the animal itself: ri-dwags 'á-dzi-nai págs-pa (ri dwags 'a dzi nai pags pa) Stg.
1. col. for žaṅ-po (zhang po) mother's brother, *'a-žaṅ-tsá-wo* ('a zhang tsa wo) nephew. — 2. Cs.: 'an address' (?).
a medicinal herb S.g.?
Ssk. ('whirl, whirlpool, eddy') a disease of the rluṅ (rlung), q.v.; perh. dizziness? Med.
1. Sch.: it is of no consequence, it does not matter. — 2. n. of a plant = bya-po-tsi-tsi (bya po tsi tsi).
dead-nettle Med.
C. (= ku-yu (ku yu)) hornless, of cattle.
beard Ts.
